Home.lede: A free crowdtesting portal for indie developers — real-device beta testing and usability testing by human testers. No subscriptions, no hidden fees.
Home.step1_text: Add your app link, target platforms, a test scenario, and a few questions. It is free.
Home.step2_text: Human testers run your beta on their own phones and tablets — real usability testing, not bots.
Home.step3_text: Read real-world answers, comments, and links from actual users.

HowItWorks.title: How it works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Free crowdtesting for indie developers and human testers. No subscriptions, no hidden fees.
HowItWorks.step1_text: A developer submits an app for free: app link, target platforms, a test scenario, and a few simple questions.
HowItWorks.step2_text: Human testers browse open apps and complete the scenario on their own phones and tablets.
HowItWorks.step3_text: Developers receive real-world usability feedback from actual people — answers, comments, and links.

Faq.title: FAQ
Faq.a1: Yes. Submitting apps and testing them is free. No subscriptions, no hidden fees.
Faq.q2: How do testers get the app?
Faq.a2: Developers share a link (App Store, Google Play, TestFlight, or web). We do not host file uploads.
Faq.a3: Signed-in testers complete an app test scenario on their own device and submit answers plus comments.
Faq.a4: Developers can give a tester a +1 for a helpful report. Ratings build up a tester reputation over time.
Faq.a5: Any app, result, or review can be reported. Content is hidden automatically after enough reports.

About.meta_description: Mystery Customer Insight is a non-profit crowdtesting portal that makes real-people, real-device app testing free and accessible to every indie developer.
About.hero_subtitle: We believe an indie developer deserves the same honest, real-world feedback that big studios pay thousands for. So we built a place where anyone can get it — for free.
About.why_p1: Getting an app in front of real users used to mean an enterprise QA contract, a paid tester panel, or begging friends for feedback. Real usability testing — humans, real devices, honest answers — sat behind a price tag most indie developers simply cannot justify.
About.why_p2: Mystery Customer Insight removes that barrier. A developer submits an app with a short scenario and a few questions; human testers run it on their own phones and tablets and report back what actually happened. No subscriptions, no hidden fees, no gatekeeping.
About.why_p3: The platform is operated by the non-profit NGO “POGOVORIMO”. We are not here to sell testing seats — we are here to make honest product feedback a public good for the people who build software on their own.

principles.title: Free, and staying that way
principles.text: No subscriptions, no per-tester fees, no paywalled reports. Submitting an app and reading the results costs nothing — that is the whole point.

principles.title: Real people, real devices
principles.text: Every test is run by a human on their own phone or tablet — not bots, not emulators, not synthetic scripts. You get honest reactions from actual users.

principles.title: Built for indies
principles.text: Solo founders and small teams rarely have a QA lab or budget for a paid testing panel. We level that field so a one-person app can be tested like a funded one.

principles.title: A community, not a vendor
principles.text: Testers try new apps and their feedback genuinely shapes what ships. Developers and testers help each other — the platform just connects them.

About.cta_title: Join the people behind better apps
About.cta_subtitle: Whether you build apps or love trying new ones, there is a place for you here. It is free to start.
